description: Without rate limiting, an attacker (or a runaway client) can flood an endpoint with requests, causing Denial-of-Service, brute-force attacks, or enumeration of data.
fix_description: Apply a rate-limiting middleware (e.g. slowapi for FastAPI) to cap the number of requests per client per time window. Start conservatively on sensitive or expensive endpoints.
patch_steps: Install a rate-limiting library: pip install slowapi
Initialise the Limiter with a key function (typically the client IP).
Register the SlowAPIMiddleware with the FastAPI app.
Return HTTP 429 when the limit is exceeded (slowapi does this automatically).
verification_rules: Sending more than N requests per minute from the same IP returns HTTP 429
Rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-*) are present in responses
The limit is documented in the OpenAPI schema
references: CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
